---
title: "14-Week Schedule"
subtitle: "32 chapters · two volumes · one semester"
---

The course runs Volume I in weeks 1–7 (posing the General Enterprise Optimization
Problem) and Volume II in weeks 8–14 (solving it). The double rule marks the volume
boundary: the midterm sits exactly where the GEOP is posed. Instructors may
re-balance — every chapter page is self-contained.

```{=html}
<table class="sched">
<thead><tr><th>Week</th><th>Chapters</th><th>Theme</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr id="week-1"><td><span class="wkb">1</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch01.html">VI.1 — Introduction to Dynamic Corporate Transformation</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch02.html">VI.2 — Enterprise Systems and Transformation</a></td><td class="theme">DCT vision; enterprise systems</td></tr>
<tr id="week-2"><td><span class="wkb">2</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch03.html">VI.3 — Mathematical Foundations</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch04.html">VI.4 — Enterprise Modeling Principles</a></td><td class="theme">Mathematical foundations; modeling principles</td></tr>
<tr id="week-3"><td><span class="wkb">3</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch05.html">VI.5 — Enterprise State Representation</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch06.html">VI.6 — Enterprise Transformation Operators</a></td><td class="theme">Enterprise state; transformation operators</td></tr>
<tr id="week-4"><td><span class="wkb">4</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch07.html">VI.7 — Dynamic Enterprise Systems</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch08.html">VI.8 — Stochastic Enterprise Dynamics</a></td><td class="theme">Dynamic systems; stochastic dynamics</td></tr>
<tr id="week-5"><td><span class="wkb">5</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch09.html">VI.9 — Enterprise State Architecture</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch10.html">VI.10 — Enterprise Capital Architecture</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch11.html">VI.11 — Enterprise Performance Architecture</a></td><td class="theme">The architectures: state, capital, performance</td></tr>
<tr id="week-6"><td><span class="wkb">6</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch12.html">VI.12 — Enterprise Risk, Resilience, and Robustness Architecture</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch13.html">VI.13 — Unified Enterprise Transformation Architecture</a></td><td class="theme">Risk, resilience, robustness; UETA</td></tr>
<tr id="week-7" class="boundary"><td><span class="wkb">7</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v1ch14.html">VI.14 — Mathematical Analysis of the Unified Enterprise Transformation Architecture</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch15.html">VI.15 — Mathematical Formulation of Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v1ch16.html">VI.16 — Synthesis, Future Directions, and Transition to Volume II</a></td><td class="theme">Analysis of UETA; the GEOP; synthesis <br><span class="milestone">&#9670; Midterm</span></td></tr>
<tr id="week-8"><td><span class="wkb">8</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch01.html">VII.1 — Introduction to Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch02.html">VII.2 — The General Enterprise Optimization Problem Revisited</a></td><td class="theme">Enterprise optimization; GEOP revisited</td></tr>
<tr id="week-9"><td><span class="wkb">9</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch03.html">VII.3 — Convex Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch04.html">VII.4 — Nonlinear Enterprise Optimization</a></td><td class="theme">Convex; nonlinear</td></tr>
<tr id="week-10"><td><span class="wkb">10</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch05.html">VII.5 — Dynamic Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch06.html">VII.6 — Optimal Control of Enterprise Systems</a></td><td class="theme">Dynamic optimization; optimal control (PMP)</td></tr>
<tr id="week-11"><td><span class="wkb">11</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch07.html">VII.7 — Dynamic Programming for Enterprise Systems</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch08.html">VII.8 — Hamilton--Jacobi--Bellman Enterprise Framework</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch09.html">VII.9 — Stochastic Enterprise Optimization</a></td><td class="theme">The Bellman arc: DP, HJB, stochastic control</td></tr>
<tr id="week-12"><td><span class="wkb">12</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch10.html">VII.10 — Neuro-Fuzzy Robust Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch11.html">VII.11 — Distributionally Robust Enterprise Optimization</a></td><td class="theme">The robustness pair: NF-REO, DRO</td></tr>
<tr id="week-13"><td><span class="wkb">13</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch12.html">VII.12 — Multi-Objective Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch13.html">VII.13 — Machine Learning for Enterprise Transformation</a></td><td class="theme">Multi-objective; machine learning</td></tr>
<tr id="week-14"><td><span class="wkb">14</span></td><td><a href="chapters/v2ch14.html">VII.14 — Artificial Intelligence for Enterprise Optimization</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch15.html">VII.15 — Enterprise Digital Twins and Autonomous Enterprise Transformation</a><br><a href="chapters/v2ch16.html">VII.16 — Enterprise Applications and Integrated Transformation Case Studies</a></td><td class="theme">AI; digital twins; case-study capstone <br><span class="milestone">&#9670; Final</span></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
```
